name: Glassfang Stalker
shortDesc: A bipedal predator whose mutated hide regrew as Gemosphere quartz, refracting light into hypnotic patterns.
fullDesc:
First catalogued by Field Team Echo near the eastern Scorched Wastes, the Glassfang Stalker is a post-apocalyptic descendant of canid mammals that integrated trace Gemosphere crystals into its keratin during gestation. Its plating is functionally translucent and acts as a passive optical lure: prey approaching at dusk perceive the creature as a shimmering pool of water.

GEC protocol mandates Tactical Analysis: long-range observation to identify Fracture Points (typically the unarmored throat plating) before any engagement. Approach protocol DL-4 must be observed at all times.

discussions.author: field.echo
discussions.text: Visual lure is active at dusk. @j.huang, please prepare sensor filters.

replies.author: j.huang
replies.text: Optical filter package pushed to all visors. @field.echo, please verify v2.3.

fieldNotes.title: Glassfang Stalker Survey Log
fieldNotes.body: Perimeter readings are stable, with minor resonance drift after dusk. Routine monitoring remains in effect.

fieldNotes.title: Glassfang Stalker Handling Directive
fieldNotes.body: All personnel must confirm sensor calibration before direct interaction and file a post-shift anomaly summary.

observations.title: Glassfang Stalker Observation
observations.body: Observed behavior aligns with current doctrine; no escalation required beyond standing protocols.

name: Hollow Choir
habitat: Deep ruins beneath former metropolitan zones
shortDesc: A swarm-mind entity that mimics human voices to draw survivors deeper into collapsed Jewerlian shrines.
fullDesc:
The Hollow Choir is not a single creature but a colonial intelligence formed when Jewerlian vocal-print fragments bonded to mutated locust biomass during the Final Resonance. Field recordings describe choirs of stolen voices - children, partners, fallen comrades - emanating from sealed substructures.

The Choir shifts vocal frequency the moment a counter-signal is broadcast, defeating most acoustic countermeasures. Doctrine therefore mandates High-Intensity Suppression: incendiary saturation before the swarm completes its adaptive cycle. All encounters are designated Code Black.

[L3+]Identified Fracture Point: the colonial nucleus collapses when exposed to a sustained 14.7 kHz Jewerlian counter-resonance for ≥ 9 seconds, provided the broadcast originates from at least three spatially separated emitters. Field teams holding L3+ clearance may requisition the MK-VII triad rig from Armory West. Any attempt below the three-emitter threshold has resulted in 100% operative loss in past trials (see incident dossiers HC-04 through HC-09).[/L3+]

name: Mossback Drifter
habitat: Verdant Hollows and other Fae-touched forests
shortDesc: A docile herbivore whose carapace hosts living moss colonies, often used by Fae-touched as pack animals.
fullDesc:
The Mossback Drifter is one of the few large mutated species considered fully benign. Its dorsal carapace is colonized by symbiotic moss that filters airborne Gemosphere particulates, making the creature a walking air-purifier in sealed habitats.

GEC protocol is Routine Survey only - elimination is not authorized unless a herd obstructs an active expansion path. Drifters bond readily with handlers and have been integrated into Verdant Hollow caravans as low-impact pack animals. No incident has been recorded in 47 years of cohabitation.

name: Resonant Vessel
shortDesc: A dormant crystalline construct that amplifies Gemosphere energy when properly attuned, repurposed by Morneven as a power source.
fullDesc:
The Resonant Vessel is not a living entity in the traditional sense, but rather a crystalline formation that emerged when concentrated Gemosphere deposits fused with decayed Jewerlian machinery. Unlike hostile anomalies, the Vessel exhibits a symbiotic relationship with controlled energy fields - it absorbs ambient resonance and redirects it in stable, harvestable pulses.

Morneven Institute Directive: Vessels are designated as Strategic Assets under Protocol CATALYST-7. Field teams are authorized to locate, secure, and transport intact specimens to Institute facilities for integration into power generation systems. Destruction is prohibited without Executive override. All Vessels are logged as renewable resource nodes rather than threats.

name: Annihilation Pattern Theta
shortDesc: A self-propagating energy phenomenon that consumes all matter and energy in its radius, expanding exponentially if left unchecked.
fullDesc:
Annihilation Pattern Theta represents the absolute failure category of the GEC system - an entity that cannot be contained, negotiated with, or weaponized. It manifests as a geometric void approximately 3 meters in diameter, surrounded by a corona of inverted light. Anything crossing its event horizon - matter, energy, even Gemosphere resonance - ceases to exist.

Morneven Doctrine is explicit: Singularity-class entities are Existential Threats requiring Immediate Neutralization. All available resources, including tactical nuclear assets and Fracture-Point teams, are authorized for deployment. Theta's containment zone is maintained by a perpetual bombardment of counter-resonance fields at the cost of 12 Institute operatives per quarter. If containment fails, Gemora's eastern hemisphere will be uninhabitable within 72 hours.

name: Echo Remnant
shortDesc: The inert residue of a previously active anomaly, now harmless but catalogued for historical and research purposes.
fullDesc:
Echo Remnants are the terminal state of Gemora anomalies that have been successfully neutralized through Morneven intervention. They appear as faintly luminescent crystalline dust or ashen formations, devoid of the energetic signatures that defined their active states. No behavioral patterns, no threat assessment, no Fracture Points - only the archaeological record remains.

GEC Protocol ZS-1 applies: Zero-State entities are Closed Files. They require no tactical response, no observation protocols, and no resource allocation. Remnants are marked on survey maps solely to prevent redundant investigation by field teams. The presence of a Zero-State marker indicates a successful operation and the permanent elimination of a threat to human survival.

name: Tideglass Leviathan
habitat: Submerged coastal trenches - former Pacific basin
shortDesc: A kilometer-scale aquatic apex predator whose body refracts sonar into false echoes, making it nearly invisible to fleet sensors.
fullDesc:
The Tideglass Leviathan is the largest confirmed Crystalline-class entity in the catalogue. Its body is composed of layered Gemosphere quartz suspended in a living connective matrix, allowing it to refract not only light but also sonar pulses, producing dozens of false returns on any active sensor sweep.

Morneven shipping lanes route around its known migration corridors. Engagement is not authorized below DL-4 and requires Director sign-off. The entity has not initiated hostilities against Institute vessels in seventeen years, but doctrine assumes that pattern is not a guarantee.

[L3+]The Leviathan's only confirmed Fracture Point is a 14-meter-wide cardiac aperture that opens for approximately 2.3 seconds during deep-dive descent. Any strike must be delivered through the aperture during that window. Three confirmed kills in recorded history; all three were delivered by Sola without the use of Solar's Lens.[/L3+]

name: Pale Surveyor
shortDesc: A humanoid mimic that copies the silhouette and gait of recently deceased operatives to approach standing checkpoints.
fullDesc:
The Pale Surveyor is a soft-tissue Metamorphic that imprints on the body language of any human or demi-human it observes for more than ninety consecutive seconds. It uses the imprinted gait and posture to approach checkpoints, often impersonating operatives reported missing or killed within the previous 30 days.

It does not speak; it mimics silhouette only. The standard counter-protocol is the 'three-question gate' - a rotating set of personnel-specific questions never recorded in writing. Any approach failing the gate is to be engaged immediately.

The Surveyor's body adapts within seconds when struck, shifting its mass to absorb impact. High-Intensity Suppression doctrine applies: it must be neutralized before it completes the second adaptation cycle (~4 seconds).

name: Lantern Moth Bloom
habitat: Verdant Hollow canopy and Citadel sky-gardens
shortDesc: A migrating cloud of bioluminescent moths whose wing-dust mildly destabilizes nearby anomalies - useful, but irritating to personnel.
fullDesc:
Lantern Moth Blooms are seasonal swarms of small bioluminescent moths whose wing-scales contain trace counter-resonance compounds. A passing bloom will weaken nearby Crystalline and Metamorphic activity by an average of 11% for several hours.

The blooms are non-aggressive and are deliberately encouraged by Verdant Hollow caretakers. The DL-2 rating reflects only respiratory irritation in unmasked personnel and an elevated risk of small electrical fires when the bloom passes through unsealed switchgear. Field teams are issued mesh hoods during bloom season.

name: Heliograph Spire
habitat: High-altitude plateaus exposed to direct Gemosphere refraction
shortDesc: A self-erecting crystal tower that captures violet-dawn radiation and releases it as steady directional energy beams.
fullDesc:
The Heliograph Spire is a slow-growing Catalyst-class formation that erects itself over decades on plateaus with consistent Gemosphere exposure. Once mature, it captures the violet-dawn refraction event each morning and slowly releases the stored energy across the day as a tightly collimated beam.

Morneven uses mature Spires as off-grid power relays for remote outposts. The DL-3 rating reflects the genuine hazard of standing within the beam's projection arc - the discharge is invisible to unaugmented vision and has flash-vaporized at least two unbriefed handlers. Project SOLSTICE maintains a registry of all known Spires and their beam vectors.
